🧭 CC Parallel Primitives — Decision Tool

Claude Code gives you three different parallel primitives. They're not interchangeable. This page helps you pick the right one for your workload. Companion to docs/parallel-primitives.md.

Task tool

STAR pattern · subagents · in-process

~150K tok simplest

  • N independent things in parallel
  • Parent collects results
  • No agent-to-agent chat
  • Dies with parent session

Example: /ork:cover spawns one Task per test tier.

Agent Teams

MESH pattern · TeamCreate + SendMessage

~400K tok debate-capable

  • Agents react to each other in real time
  • Design debate, hand-off chains
  • ~3× Task tool cost for back-and-forth
  • Dies with parent session

Example: /ork:brainstorm with system-designer vs backend-thinker.

claude agents CLI

PROCESS-ISOLATED · separate OS processes

N × budget survives /clear

  • Overnight / multi-hour batches
  • Each session has own context budget
  • Survives terminal close, /clear, reboot
  • No comms except shared filesystem

Example: M170 wave — 4 detached sessions, one per worktree.

Pick the right primitive

Q1
Do the N tasks need to talk to each other while running?
Q2
Do they need to survive `/clear`, terminal close, or run overnight?
Q3 (sanity check)
Is the work actually parallel, or is it a single loop over N items?
Verdict
—
—

Case study — today's 5-PR merge queue

PrimitiveFit?Why
Task tool❌Subagents return and die — can't watch persistently
Agent Teams❌No coordination needed — rebase is mechanical
claude agents CLI❌Heavy: N processes for a polling loop is wasteful
A single foreground loop + gh CLI✅ONE process, ONE budget, watches N PRs

Lesson: not every "N things" needs parallelism. Sometimes the right answer is a smarter single loop.

Replaces /ork:agents-view (deleted 2026-05-20). Live primitives: Task tool · Agent Teams (TeamCreate / SendMessage, CC 2.1.33+) · claude agents CLI (CC 2.1.139+).
